summary: Second-round review of CC-0090 after external-review improvements. Changes address all three items from berendt's external review #1: (W-001) Chart.yaml version bumped 0.2.0 → 0.3.0 with a new artifacthub.io/changes entry for the NetworkPolicy feature; (W-002) tests/e2e/keystone-operator/network-policy-egress/chainsaw-test.yaml gains a prominent SCOPE CAVEAT comment block that documents kindnet non-enforcement, Service-VIP DNAT on Calico/Cilium, and the leader-election ambiguity introduced by the second operator release, plus a follow-up note for a Calico-enabled kind CI job; (I-002) values.schema.json expands the 'cidr' description to document that the IPv6 regex half is intentionally loose and that final IPv6 validation is delegated to apiserver admission. Supporting .planwerk/ artefacts are consistent: progress phase moved awaiting_external_review → improving, external review JSON verdict flipped NEEDS_CHANGES → ADDRESSED, the 'update-documentation-when-referenced-code-behavior-changes' review pattern gained a CC-0090 entry (occurrences 6 → 7), and a new 'document-cnienvironment-limitations-of-network-policy-tests' review pattern was recorded. No code-level logic changes; no new risk surface introduced.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (no test code changed in this review round; helm-unittest + chainsaw suite from round 1 still in place)
Edge cases are covered (schema validation + fail-closed guards unchanged)
Chainsaw E2E test documents its own enforcement scope (kindnet caveat now inline)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing chart conventions (version bump + artifacthub.io/changes entry mirror CC-0069 precedent)
No scope creep — changes strictly target the three items flagged by berendt
Comments explain WHY (IPv6 laxity rationale, test scope caveat), not WHAT

security_checklist.item: No new user inputs; schema change is documentation-only (regex unchanged)
No secrets, no auth surface, no new egress paths introduced

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — pure metadata + doc-comment edits
Chart version bump correctly follows semver for a new top-level values key (networkPolicy)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code introduced; no new abstractions

fail_fast_checklist.item: Pre-existing fail-closed guards in templates/networkpolicy.yaml untouched

defensive_checklist.item: Schema still validates CIDR pattern, port range, and boolean type
IPv6 laxity now explicitly documented — no hidden assumption

suggested_improvements: Track the follow-up from the chainsaw-test SCOPE CAVEAT as an explicit ticket: a Calico- or Cilium-enabled kind CI job that re-runs this test under an enforcing CNI, ideally with a negative assertion that traffic to a non-allow-listed destination is blocked. Without that, the egress allow-list remains validated only by helm-unittest rendering, not by runtime enforcement.

Consider adding a brief link from docs/reference/keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md to the same SCOPE CAVEAT so operators reading the reference doc see the enforcement caveat without having to open the Chainsaw test file.
next_steps: Merge: all three external-review items are addressed and no new issues were introduced by the fixes.
Open a follow-up ticket for the Calico-enabled kind CI job referenced in the chainsaw-test SCOPE CAVEAT (tracked intent, not currently scheduled).
